<!-- source-hash: c59cfdde3e5b9e8af02e692f63af6bfd -->
Response DTO representing a tag assigned to a device, encapsulating both tag definition metadata (key, description, color) and the specific values assigned to a particular device.

## Key Components

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `tagId` | `String` | Unique identifier of the tag definition |
| `key` | `String` | Tag key identifier (e.g., `"site"`) |
| `description` | `String` | Human-readable tag description |
| `color` | `String` | Hex color code for UI display |
| `values` | `List<String>` | Tag values assigned to the device |
| `createdAt` | `Instant` | Timestamp of tag assignment |
| `createdBy` | `String` | User ID who assigned the tag |

## Usage Example

```java
// Building a DeviceTagResponse from a tag assignment
DeviceTagResponse response = DeviceTagResponse.builder()
    .tagId("tag-456")
    .key("site")
    .description("Physical location of the device")
    .color("#FF5733")
    .values(List.of("site1", "site2"))
    .createdAt(Instant.now())
    .createdBy("user-789")
    .build();

// Typical usage in a controller returning device tags
List<DeviceTagResponse> tags = deviceTagService.getTagsForDevice(deviceId);
return ResponseEntity.ok(tags);
```

> This DTO is part of the OpenFrame external API layer (`com.openframe.external.dto.device`) and is annotated with OpenAPI/Swagger schema metadata for automatic API documentation generation. It uses Lombok to eliminate boilerplate for getters, setters, constructors, and the builder pattern.
